steps.title: Convert the radical to exponential form
steps.content:
The right side contains $\sqrt[3]{4^7}$. Using the rule $\sqrt[n]{x} = x^{1/n}$, we rewrite:
$$\sqrt[3]{4^7} = (4^7)^{1/3} = 4^{7/3}$$

steps.title: Equate exponents with the same base
steps.content:
The equation now reads:
$$4^{8c} = 4^{7/3}$$
When exponential expressions have the same base, their exponents are equal:
$$8c = \frac{7}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 8:
$$c = \frac{7}{3 \cdot 8} = \frac{7}{24}$$
As a decimal, $c \approx 0.2917$.
